interpretation: Fair — significant data quality issues detected

recommended_actions.action: Investigate duplicate records. Add dedup logic keyed on non-key fields.
Fix null values in required column 'tenant_id' at the source.
Investigate root cause of vacancy_loss discrepancy for 2025-12. Check for duplicate events, missing data, or filter logic differences.
Investigate root cause of delinquency discrepancy for 2025-12. Check for duplicate events, missing data, or filter logic differences.

top_risks.business_impact: LP distributions calculated on fabricated 5.1% vacancy loss — actual vacancy is 0.0%. Investors received distributions based on false occupancy assumptions
top_risks.title: vacancy_loss misreported for 2025-12 (off by +100.0%)

top_risks.business_impact: Delinquency understated to investors at 2.7% — actual is 2.9%, masking $200K+ in uncollected rent
top_risks.title: delinquency misreported for 2025-12 (off by -8.7%)

top_risks.business_impact: 60 duplicate property records inflating portfolio size metrics reported to LPs
top_risks.title: Near-duplicate rows detected

top_risks.business_impact: 32 leases missing tenant_id — cannot track delinquency or vacancy at the tenant level
top_risks.title: Null values in required column: tenant_id

executive_summary: BayesIQ audits a real estate firm's portfolio performance reporting across vacancy tracking, tenant delinquency, and collection rates. The audit surfaces 4 issues — including a complete vacancy loss fabrication (reported 5.1%, actual 0.0%), null tenant IDs breaking lease-level aggregations, and 60 duplicate property records. LP distributions had been calculated on fundamentally incorrect occupancy assumptions. After remediation, the reliability score improves from 43 to 65.
